json.comment: Synthetic HCV-MZL reference case per specs/REFERENCE_CASE_SPECIFICATION.md §2. NOT real patient data — fully synthesized for acceptance testing of the §1.3 critical structural criteria. Engine-required fields at top; the clinical_record block carries the spec-§2 patient profile template populated with realistic synthetic values, kept alongside so render layers and downstream tooling can read it.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: HCV-associated marginal zone lymphoma
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Extranodal marginal zone lymphoma of mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue
primary_diagnosis.biopsy_site: root of tongue with tonsil involvement

primary_lesion.location: root of tongue with tonsil involvement

symptoms_and_concerns.symptoms_onset: approximately 6 months — gradually enlarging tongue-base mass, mild dysphagia

json.comment: Synthetic HCV-positive MZL with high tumor burden + B-symptoms. Engine should select aggressive track — concurrent DAA + BR (IND-HCV-MZL-1L-BR-AGGRESSIVE).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: HCV-associated marginal zone lymphoma
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Indolent B-cell lymphomas — Marginal zone lymphomas — HCV-associated MZL

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: HCV-associated marginal zone lymphoma

json.comment: Synthetic newly-diagnosed MM patient with standard-risk cytogenetics. Engine should select VRd (standard track) as default.

json.comment: Synthetic newly-diagnosed MM with high-risk cytogenetics (t(4;14) + gain 1q21). Engine should select D-VRd (aggressive track) as default.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Multiple myeloma, IgA lambda

suspicion.presentation: Splenomegaly + mediastinal lymphadenopathy + cytopenias + B-symptoms (drenching night sweats x 6 weeks)

json._comment: Same patient as patient_diagnostic_lymphoma_suspect.json, but biopsy came back: HCV-MZL confirmed. For testing diagnostic→treatment promotion (DiagnosticPlan superseded by Plan v1).

json.comment: Synthetic newly-diagnosed DLBCL NOS with low IPI (0-1). Engine should select R-CHOP (standard track) as default.

primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Aggressive B-cell lymphomas — Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, NOS

json.comment: Synthetic newly-diagnosed DLBCL NOS with high IPI (3+, age >60, advanced stage, multiple extranodal). Engine should select Pola-R-CHP (aggressive track) as default.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma, NOS
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Aggressive B-cell lymphomas — Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, NOS

funding_pathway.notes: Без підтвердженого charitable / clinical-trial / out-of-pocket pathway пацієнта спрямовано на R-CHOP як fallback

json.comment: FL grade 1-2, asymptomatic, no GELF criteria → engine should select WATCH (surveillance) as default.
FL grade 1-2 with high tumor burden by GELF (8 cm mass + B-symptoms) → engine should select BR (standard) as default.
FL with rapid progression + LDH doubling → transformation suspect → engine should select R-CHOP (aggressive).
CLL with iwCLL indication, standard-risk genetics (no TP53/del 17p/IGHV-unmutated). Engine should select BTKi continuous (acalabrutinib) as default.
CLL with TP53 mutation + IGHV-unmutated + complex karyotype. Engine should select VenO fixed-duration as default.
MCL fit younger (age 58, ECOG 0, TP53-wt). Engine should select intensive R-CHOP/R-DHAP+autoSCT as default.
MCL with TP53 mutation + age 71 + ECOG 2 (multiple reasons to go BTKi+R). Engine should select REG-ACALABRUTINIB-RITUXIMAB.
SMZL with HCV-positive status, compensated liver. Engine should select DAA antiviral (HCV-positive track) as default.
SMZL HCV-negative. Engine should select rituximab monotherapy as default.
Nodal MZL, asymptomatic, no GELF criteria. Engine should select W&W (surveillance).
Burkitt without high-risk features (no CNS, LDH within bounds, no leukemic phase). Engine should select DA-EPOCH-R standard.
Burkitt with CNS involvement + LDH >3× ULN + bulky abdomen. RF-BURKITT-HIGH-RISK fired. Engine should select CODOX-M/IVAC aggressive.
Typical HCL with cytopenia + splenomegaly. Engine should select cladribine.
WM MYD88 L265P+ with iwWM treatment indication (anemia + IgM neuropathy). Engine should select zanubrutinib.
HGBL-DH (MYC + BCL2 break-apart). Engine should select DA-EPOCH-R.
Systemic ALCL ALK-negative (worse prognosis subtype, universally CD30+). Engine should select CHP-Bv (ECHELON-2) as default.
PTCL NOS, CD30-negative. Engine should select CHOEP standard (CHP-Bv would be alternative if CD30+).
AITL with CD30+. Engine should select CHP-Bv (RF-TCELL-CD30-POSITIVE fired).
Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma stage IV. Engine should select A+AVD as default.
Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma stage IIA. Engine should select ABVD as default.
NLPBL stage IA, localized. Engine should select observation/RT as default (surveillance track).
AITL with CD30-negative biopsy. Engine should select CHOEP standard (RF-TCELL-CD30-POSITIVE not fired). AITL-specific workup adds EBER-ISH + IgG quant + DAT.
MF stage IB (skin patches/plaques without nodal/blood involvement). Engine should default to skin-directed (no systemic regimen). Sezary count B0, no LCT.
Sézary syndrome (B2 leukemic phase, generalized erythroderma). Engine should select mogamulizumab — RF-MF-SEZARY-LEUKEMIC fires step 1 then step 2 routes to MOGA (CD30-).
Advanced MF stage IIB with large-cell transformation, CD30+. Engine should select brentuximab vedotin monotherapy (LCT triggers step 1, CD30+ triggers step 2 → BV-mono).
NLPBL stage IA in young woman with cervical adenopathy — RT contraindicated due to breast/thyroid field overlap concerns. Engine should select rituximab monotherapy alternative.
Synthetic chemorefractory DLBCL post 2 prior lines (R-CHOP → R-ICE failed). Age 58, fit ECOG 1. Engine should select IND-DLBCL-3L-AXICEL-CART via line_of_therapy=3 + CD20+/CD19+ + ECOG 0-1.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma, NOS — chemorefractory

json.comment: Synthetic relapsed DLBCL post R-CHOP at 8 mo, transplant-ineligible (age 72, ECOG 2). Engine should select IND-DLBCL-2L-POLA-R-BENDAMUSTINE via line_of_therapy=2 + CD20+ + transplant-ineligible profile.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma, NOS — relapsed

transplant_assessment.rationale: Age 72 + ECOG 2 + comorbidity (cardiac, mild renal) — auto-SCT not recommended

json.comment: Synthetic HGBL-DH (MYC + BCL2 rearrangement) primary-refractory to 1L DA-EPOCH-R, fit for CAR-T. Engine should route to 2L axi-cel CAR-T (IND-HGBL-DH-2L-CART-AXICEL) as aggressive track.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: High-Grade B-Cell Lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangement (double-hit)
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Aggressive B-cell lymphomas — High-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangement

json.comment: Synthetic typical PMBCL — young female, mediastinal mass, no superior vena cava syndrome. Engine should select DA-EPOCH-R (IND-PMBCL-1L-DA-EPOCH-R) as standard track.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Mediastinal (Thymic) Large B-Cell Lymphoma
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Aggressive B-cell lymphomas — Primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma

json.comment: Synthetic PMBCL with bulky mediastinal mass + superior vena cava syndrome. Engine should select DA-EPOCH-R (preferred for bulky/aggressive PMBCL) and flag urgent SVC management.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Mediastinal (Thymic) Large B-Cell Lymphoma

json.comment: Synthetic PMBCL relapsed 14 months post-1L R-CHOP+RT, transplant-eligible. Engine should select 2L R-ICE → autoSCT (IND-PMBCL-2L-RICE-AUTOSCT).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Mediastinal (Thymic) Large B-Cell Lymphoma — relapsed

json.comment: Synthetic typical PCNSL — fit middle-aged patient, single periventricular lesion. Engine should select R-MPV induction (IND-PCNSL-1L-R-MPV) as standard, with consolidation pathway (autoSCT thiotepa-based).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma of the CNS
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Aggressive B-cell lymphomas — Primary CNS DLBCL

json.comment: Synthetic elderly frail PCNSL — age 78, ECOG 2, CrCl 48 (HD-MTX-marginal). Engine should flag frailty (RF-PCNSL-FRAILTY-AGE) and consider reduced-intensity HD-MTX (1 g/m²) or rituximab + temozolomide; route to alternative track if HD-MTX excluded.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma of the CNS

json.comment: Synthetic atypical PCNSL — immunocompromised (post-renal-transplant), EBV-positive lymphoma. PCNSL in immunocompromised setting overlaps PTLD pathway — engine should flag for MDT review (immunosuppression reduction first, then rituximab-based therapy).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary CNS DLBCL (EBV-positive, immunocompromised setting)
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: EBV-positive DLBCL of the CNS — immunocompromised host

staging.stage: Brain only — periventricular + corpus callosum involvement

json.comment: Synthetic CLL post-acalabrutinib 18-mo progression with BTK-C481-mut detected and prior venetoclax exposure. Engine should select IND-CLL-3L-PIRTOBRUTINIB via line_of_therapy=3 + post-cBTKi.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia — post-cBTKi progression

json.comment: Synthetic relapsed BRAF-V600E HCL after 1L cladribine, durable initial response (5 years), now relapse. Engine should select vemurafenib + rituximab (IND-HCL-2L-VEMURAFENIB) as 2L preferred.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Hairy Cell Leukemia — relapsed

json.comment: Synthetic Waldenström macroglobulinemia post-rituximab progression with CXCR4-WHIM-mut detected and pre-existing baseline grade 2 IgM-related neuropathy. Engine should select IND-WM-2L-CARFILZOMIB-CXCR4MUT via line_of_therapy=2 + BIO-CXCR4-WHIM positive.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Waldenström Macroglobulinemia (Lymphoplasmacytic Lymphoma) — relapsed

neurological.igm_paraneuropathy: grade 2 sensory peripheral neuropathy at baseline — bortezomib contraindicated

json.comment: Synthetic nodal MZL with high tumor burden (GELF criteria positive), symptomatic. Engine should select BR (IND-NMZL-1L-BR) over watch-and-wait.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Nodal Marginal Zone Lymphoma

json.comment: Systemic ALCL ALK-positive, fit younger patient. Engine should select CHP-Bv (ECHELON-2) as default, with BV maintenance candidate post-ASCT only if high-risk features (this case is standard-risk).
Systemic ALCL ALK-negative, relapsed after CHOEP (BV not yet given because brentuximab unfunded at 1L). Engine should route to BV monotherapy 2L per SGN-35-005, planned bridge to alloSCT.
PTCL-NOS CD30-positive (≥10% IHC), fit younger. Engine should select CHP-Bv (ECHELON-2 inclusion criterion met).
PTCL-NOS relapsed after CHOEP + autoSCT, HDACi-naive, romidepsin accessible. Engine should select romidepsin 2L (default per algo) bridging to alloSCT consideration.
AITL relapsed 14 months after CHOEP + autoSCT, HDACi-naive, TET2/DNMT3A epigenetic profile. Engine should route to azacitidine 2L (default per algo, AITL-specific epigenetic rationale).
Typical EATL: refractory celiac disease patient, fit ≤70, jejunal mass with multifocal enteropathy. Engine should select CHOEP + autoSCT consolidation (1L standard).
EATL relapsed after CHOEP induction; not yet transplanted. Engine should route to ICE-style 2L salvage with bridge-to-alloSCT plan.
HSTCL fit younger patient with potential matched donor — engine should select ICE + alloSCT consolidation pathway (aggressive track).
Iatrogenic HSTCL after long-term anti-TNF + azathioprine for Crohn's disease. Engine should select ICE + alloSCT pathway AND surface immediate cessation of anti-TNF + thiopurines via MDT brief.
Typical T-PLL: marked lymphocytosis, hepatosplenomegaly, CD52-bright. Engine should select alemtuzumab IV 1L with alloSCT consolidation in fit.
T-PLL refractory to alemtuzumab IV induction (no CR, persistent disease at 12 weeks). Engine should route to venetoclax + alemtuzumab combination 2L bridging to alloSCT.
Aggressive (acute) ATLL with hypercalcemia and high LDH in HTLV-1+ patient. Engine should select aggressive 1L pathway (CHOEP + mogamulizumab + alloSCT planning).
Indolent smoldering ATLL: mild lymphocytosis, asymptomatic skin lesions, no organ dysfunction, normal LDH. Engine should select AZT + IFN-α 1L (indolent track).
NK/T-cell nasal lymphoma stage IIA, fit younger, asparaginase accessible. Engine should select SMILE 1L + concurrent RT 50 Gy.
NK/T-cell nasal lymphoma relapsed 6 months after SMILE + RT, EBV viral load rising. Engine should select PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor (avelumab) 2L bridging to alloSCT.
Advanced MF stage IIIA, relapsed after mogamulizumab induction with PR. Engine should select bexarotene 2L (mogamulizumab already exhausted, not CD30+).
Polymorphic CD20+ EBV-driven PTLD post-renal-transplant 4 years prior. Engine should select reduce-IS + rituximab 1L (default). On response, route to PTLD-1 sequential maintenance.
Monomorphic DLBCL-like CD20+ PTLD post-cardiac-transplant 7 years prior, high-risk biology. Engine should select R-CHOP 1L (aggressive track per high-risk red flag).

label: cHL · AYA Stage IIB Bulky Mediastinal

json.comment: Synthetic pediatric/AYA cHL stage IIB (age 17) with bulky mediastinal mass + B-symptoms. Engine should propose adult-style A+AVD + ISRT to bulky mediastinum vs pediatric OEPA-COPDAC (EuroNet-PHL) protocol with response-adapted RT. 3rd cHL fixture (pediatric/AYA bulky-mediastinal scenario distinct from early ABVD, advanced N+AVD, and relapsed-for-salvage).
json.imaging_summary: Contrast CT chest: 11.5 cm anterior mediastinal mass with MMR 0.42 (bulky by GHSG criteria), bilateral hilar adenopathy, no pulmonary infiltrate; PET-CT: avid mediastinum (SUVmax 18) + bilateral hilar + low cervical, Deauville baseline 5; no infradiaphragmatic disease.

patient_preferences.concerns_about_radiation: discussed risk of secondary breast malignancy with bulky mediastinal RT

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma (nodular sclerosis subtype), stage IIB, bulky mediastinal mass (11.5 cm, MMR 0.42), B-symptoms — adolescent

diagnosis.ghsg_risk_group: intermediate (HD17-style: bulky mediastinum upgrades to advanced-equivalent treatment)

fitness_assessment.rationale: AYA (17 y) — choice between adult RATHL/AHL2011 framework (A+AVD + response-adapted ISRT to bulky mediastinum) vs pediatric EuroNet-PHL (OEPA-COPDAC ± ISRT). Both arms need fertility preservation (done) and minimisation of cardiopulmonary RT exposure (proton therapy if available).

treatment_planning_notes: Pediatric protocol favors avoiding bleomycin-cumulative pulmonary tox in long survivorship
Adult A+AVD backbone (ECHELON-1) avoids bleomycin already and has PFS benefit
ISRT to mediastinum at lowest effective dose if PET-2 Deauville ≤3

json.comment: Synthetic cHL relapsed at 8 mo post A+AVD, ECOG 1, transplant-eligible. Engine should select salvage (ICE/DHAP) → ASCT pathway and post-ASCT brentuximab vedotin maintenance (IND-CHL-2L-BRENTUXIMAB-MAINTENANCE).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma, relapsed at 8 months post A+AVD

json.comment: Synthetic MM post-VRd induction (PR), M-spike rising at 12 mo, lenalidomide-refractory, fit ECOG 1. Engine should select IND-MM-2L-DKD via line_of_therapy=2 + len-refractory profile.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Multiple myeloma, IgG kappa — relapsed

json.comment: Synthetic triple-class refractory MM at 4L (post VRd → DKd → IsaKRD). Age 61, ECOG 1. Engine should select IND-MM-4L-TECLISTAMAB via line_of_therapy=4 + triple-class refractory + no active infection.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Multiple myeloma, IgA lambda — triple-class refractory

json.comment: Synthetic fit AML 1L patient, ELN intermediate-risk, NPM1+/FLT3-WT. Engine should select IND-AML-1L-7-3 (aggressive track).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Acute Myeloid Leukemia, NPM1-mutated, FLT3-WT, de novo

json.comment: Synthetic AML 1L patient age 78 with COPD + CKD3 + ECOG 2 — not eligible for intensive 7+3. Engine should select IND-AML-1L-VEN-AZA (standard-track unfit pathway).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Acute Myeloid Leukemia, secondary to MDS background — unfit for intensive induction

fitness_assessment.rationale: Age 78 + COPD + CrCl 38 + ECOG 2 → triggers unfit-track per IPSS-R / Ferrara unfit criteria

json.comment: Synthetic AML post 7+3 → CR1 → relapse with re-tested FLT3-ITD+. Age 55, ECOG 1, fit. Engine should select IND-AML-2L-GILTERITINIB-FLT3 via line_of_therapy=2 + FLT3 mutation.

findings.prior_regimen: 7+3 induction → CR1, no consolidation HCT yet

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Acute Myeloid Leukemia — FLT3-ITD-mutated, first relapse

json.comment: Synthetic APL low-risk (Sanz: WBC 2.4K, plt 60K) — should select IND-APL-1L-ATRA-ATO (chemo-free).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia, PML-RARα+, Sanz low-risk

label: APL · High Risk + DIC (ATRA + ATO + Idarubicin)

json.comment: Synthetic APL high-risk (WBC 18K, FLT3-ITD+) with active DIC — engine should select IND-APL-1L-ATRA-ATO-IDA (high-risk pathway with idarubicin or gemtuzumab) and trigger DIC red-flag.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia, PML-RARα+ (bcr1), Sanz high-risk, FLT3-ITD+, presenting DIC

json.comment: Synthetic newly diagnosed CML chronic phase, intermediate ELTS risk, Sokal high but ELTS intermediate. Engine should select IND-CML-1L-IMATINIB or IND-CML-1L-2GEN-TKI based on patient priorities (TFR-eligibility, comorbidities).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Chronic Myeloid Leukemia, chronic phase, BCR-ABL1 p210 e13a2

json.comment: Synthetic CML-CP post-imatinib + post-dasatinib failure with T315I gatekeeper mutation. Age 49, ECOG 0. Engine should select IND-CML-2L-PONATINIB-T315I via line_of_therapy=2 + T315I.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Chronic Myeloid Leukemia, BCR-ABL1+ — T315I-resistant

label: CML · Blast Crisis at Presentation (TKI + Chemo + alloHCT)

json.comment: Synthetic CML presenting de novo in myeloid blast crisis (BM blasts 38%, additional clonal evolution +8 / iAMP21). Age 44, ECOG 2. Engine should branch to high-dose imatinib OR ponatinib + AML-style induction + urgent alloHCT bridge (NCCN CML-7). 3rd CML fixture (atypical de novo advanced phase beyond chronic-phase newdx and T315I-CP).
json.imaging_summary: Abdomen US: massive splenomegaly 22 cm; no focal hepatic lesions; PET-CT: diffuse marrow uptake.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Chronic Myeloid Leukemia presenting in myeloid blast crisis (BM blasts 38%, ACA: +8, double Ph), BCR-ABL1 p210 e14a2
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Chronic myeloid leukemia, BCR::ABL1-positive — blast phase

fitness_assessment.rationale: De novo BC → urgent: high-dose imatinib 800 mg or ponatinib 45 mg + AML-style induction (e.g. 7+3 or FLAG-IDA) + immediate donor search + alloHCT consolidation

json.comment: Synthetic MDS IPSS-R high, age 62, fit, sibling donor available. Engine should select alloHCT bridging path with azacitidine until transplant via IND-MDS-HR-ALLOHCT.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: MDS, IPSS-R high-risk (5.5), ASXL1-mutated, transplant-eligible

json.comment: Synthetic MDS-HR very-high IPSS-R, age 76, ECOG 2, comorbid CHF, no donor. Engine should select IND-MDS-HR-1L-AZA (HMA monotherapy, standard track) over alloHCT or ven+aza.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: MDS, IPSS-R very-high, TP53-mutated, complex karyotype, transplant-ineligible

fitness_assessment.rationale: Age 76 + LVEF 40 + CrCl 42 + ECOG 2 → triggers HMA-only pathway

json.comment: Synthetic MDS-HR transitioning to secondary AML on f/u BM (blasts now 24%). Age 64, ECOG 1, fit, MUD identified. Engine should reroute to AML algorithm trigger (s-AML) — venetoclax + azacitidine if intensive chemo borderline, or 7+3 if fit, with immediate alloHCT bridging. 3rd MDS-HR fixture (transformation edge case beyond transplant-eligible MDS-HR and unfit-aza).

prior_regimens: Azacitidine 75 mg/m² × 7 days × 6 cycles (initial hematologic improvement, then progression)

json.imaging_summary: CT chest/abdomen: no infiltrate; spleen 13 cm; no extramedullary disease.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: MDS, IPSS-R high — progression to secondary AML (BM blasts 24%) on cycle 6 azacitidine; ASXL1+ / RUNX1+ / -7

first_line.best_response: hematologic improvement at C3, blast progression at C6 BM (16% → 24%)

fitness_assessment.rationale: Blast count >20% triggers AML algorithm reroute; fit-for-intensive but borderline → ven+aza or 7+3 reinduction → urgent alloHCT

json.comment: Synthetic MDS-LR with isolated del(5q), transfusion-dependent anemia. Engine should select IND-MDS-LR-LENALIDOMIDE-DEL5Q (high TI rate ~67% per MDS-003).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: MDS, isolated del(5q), transfusion-dependent anemia

json.comment: Synthetic MDS-LR with ring sideroblasts (SF3B1+), TD anemia, EPO 280 mU/mL (high — ESA likely to fail). Engine should select IND-MDS-LR-1L-LUSPATERCEPT (COMMAND positioning) over ESA.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: MDS with ring sideroblasts (SF3B1-mutated), transfusion-dependent, EPO-high

json.comment: Synthetic hypoplastic MDS-LR with severe pancytopenia mimicking aplastic anemia (BM cellularity 12%, no excess blasts, normal cytogenetics, HLA-DR15+). Age 38, ECOG 1. Engine should select IST pathway (horse-ATG + cyclosporine) — NCCN MDS-LR hypoplastic variant; eltrombopag add-on optional. 3rd MDS-LR fixture (atypical hypoplastic AA-overlap beyond del(5q) and RS+/EPO-high).
json.imaging_summary: CT abdomen: no organomegaly; chest CT clear; no infectious foci.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Hypoplastic MDS-LR (BM cellularity 12%, normal karyotype, no excess blasts, HLA-DR15+, small PNH clone 4%) — overlaps with aplastic anemia phenotype

ist_eligibility.rationale: Hypoplastic MDS with AA-like features → horse-ATG + cyclosporine first; eltrombopag add-on per RACE trial; alloHCT if IST fails

json.comment: Synthetic high-risk PV (age >60 + JAK2+ + prior TIA), HU-naive. Engine should select IND-PV-1L-HU + phlebotomy + low-dose ASA.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Polycythemia vera, JAK2-V617F+ (62%), high-risk (age >60 + prior TIA)

json.comment: Synthetic PV with ELN-defined HU resistance (need for phlebotomy >2× / 3 mo despite HU 2 g/d, persistent splenomegaly, severe pruritus) — engine should select IND-PV-2L-RUXOLITINIB.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Polycythemia vera, post-HU resistance (ELN), JAK2-V617F+ (75%)

first_line.regimen: HU 2 g/d × 24 months + phlebotomy + ASA 81 mg
first_line.best_response: partial — Hct control inadequate, persistent splenomegaly, intractable pruritus

json.comment: Synthetic JAK2-V617F+ PV in young woman planning pregnancy — HU contraindicated (teratogen). Engine should select pegylated interferon-alpha (peg-IFNα-2a or ropeginterferon) + low-dose ASA + phlebotomy as fertility/pregnancy-compatible cytoreduction. 3rd PV fixture (atypical reproductive-age scenario beyond high-risk HU and HU-resistant rux).
json.imaging_summary: Abdomen US: liver normal, spleen 13.5 cm (mildly enlarged); pelvic US normal.

patient_preferences.fertility_preservation_concerns: wants conception within 6–12 mo; refuses HU due to teratogenicity

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Polycythemia vera, JAK2-V617F+ (38%), reproductive-age female planning pregnancy

treatment_history.first_line_pending: peg-IFNα-2a 45–90 µg SC weekly OR ropeginterferon 100 µg q2w + ASA 81 mg + phlebotomy to Hct <45% — IFN safe through conception/pregnancy; HU absolutely contraindicated

pregnancy_specific_considerations: IFN-α has decades of safety data in MPN pregnancy
low-dose ASA continued through pregnancy (reduces miscarriage in JAK2+ PV)

json.comment: Synthetic ET high-risk (age >60 + JAK2+), platelets 920K, ECOG 0. Engine should select IND-ET-1L-HU + low-dose ASA.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Essential Thrombocythemia, JAK2-V617F+, IPSET-thrombosis high-risk

json.comment: Synthetic PMF DIPSS-Plus int-2, Hb 8.5, transfusion-dependent, post-ruxolitinib. Engine should select IND-PMF-MOMELOTINIB-ANEMIA via line_of_therapy=2 + anemia-dominant + JAKi-experienced.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Myelofibrosis — JAKi-experienced, anemia-dominant

first_line.outcome: spleen response but worsening anemia (Hb 7.8) → discontinued

json.comment: Synthetic PMF, DIPSS-Plus intermediate-2 risk, symptomatic splenomegaly + constitutional symptoms, no transplant due to age + comorbid. Engine should select IND-PMF-1L-RUXOLITINIB (COMFORT-I/II evidence).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Myelofibrosis, JAK2-V617F+, DIPSS-Plus intermediate-2, symptomatic splenomegaly

fitness_assessment.rationale: Age 71 + comorbid CKD3 + intermediate-2 (not high) → JAKi pathway, not HCT-bridge

json.comment: Synthetic high-risk PMF post-ruxolitinib failure (loss of spleen response, MIPSS70+v2 high) with massive splenomegaly + transfusion dependence in transplant-eligible patient. Engine should bridge with momelotinib OR fedratinib for spleen reduction (and anemia improvement) while activating urgent alloHCT pathway. 3rd PMF fixture (post-rux failure + transplant urgency, distinct from existing anemia-dominant momelotinib-only case).

prior_regimens: Ruxolitinib 20 mg BID × 18 months — initial spleen response then progression

json.imaging_summary: Abdomen MRI: massive splenomegaly 26 × 14 × 12 cm, spleen volume 4200 mL displacing left kidney; extensive extramedullary hematopoiesis foci in liver and paravertebral.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Primary Myelofibrosis, JAK2-V617F+ (82%), MIPSS70+v2 high (ASXL1+, SRSF2+) — post-ruxolitinib failure with massive splenomegaly + RBC transfusion dependence

first_line.outcome: loss of spleen response by month 14, worsening anemia → transfusion-dependent by month 16

fitness_assessment.rationale: Age 58 + MIPSS70+v2 high + post-rux failure → urgent alloHCT (transplant-deferring strategy no longer viable). Bridge with momelotinib (preferred — addresses spleen + anemia + symptoms) or fedratinib while donor work-up completes.

json.comment: Synthetic adult Ph+ B-ALL, age 52, ECOG 1, BCR-ABL p190+. Engine should select IND-B-ALL-1L-PH-POS (TKI + chemo backbone, e.g. dasatinib + hyper-CVAD or blinatumomab + dasatinib).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: B-cell Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia, Philadelphia chromosome positive (BCR-ABL1 p190)

json.comment: Synthetic Ph- B-ALL post-induction CR but MRD+ (1e-3) by flow cytometry. Age 32, ECOG 1, CD19+. Engine should select IND-B-ALL-BLINATUMOMAB-MRD-OR-RR via line_of_therapy=2 + MRD-positivity + CD19+.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: B-cell Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia, Ph-negative — MRD-positive post-induction

first_line.best_response: morphologic CR, MRD+ at 10^-3 by flow
first_line.next_step_pending: MRD-eradication via blinatumomab → bridge to alloHCT

json.comment: Synthetic Ph+ B-ALL relapsed after frontline imatinib + hyper-CVAD with T315I gatekeeper mutation emergent (CML-blast-crisis-like crossover). Age 41, ECOG 1. Engine should escalate to ponatinib-containing regimen + bridge to alloHCT (no other TKI active vs T315I). 3rd B-ALL fixture (atypical edge case beyond Ph+ adult / MRD+).

prior_regimens: Imatinib 600 mg + hyper-CVAD × 4 cycles (induction CR, MRD- at C2)

json.imaging_summary: PET-CT: diffuse marrow hypermetabolism, no extramedullary masses; brain MRI clear; spleen 13 cm.

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: B-cell Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia, Ph+ (BCR-ABL1 p190) — relapsed with emergent T315I after frontline imatinib + hyper-CVAD

first_line.best_response: morphologic CR with MRD- (flow, 10^-4) at C2
first_line.discontinuation_reason: hematologic relapse with emergent BCR-ABL1 T315I on kinase-domain sequencing

fitness_assessment.rationale: T315I makes imatinib/dasatinib/nilotinib/bosutinib inactive — ponatinib-based reinduction is the only TKI option; alloHCT consolidation urgent

json.comment: T-ALL relapsed after hyper-CVAD + alloSCT, no baseline neurologic deficit, nelarabine accessible. Engine should route to nelarabine 2L bridging to second alloSCT or DLI.

T-LBL with bulky mediastinal mass + airway compromise risk in young adult. Engine should select hyper-CVAD-based 1L with nelarabine consolidation; pre-induction steroids + ICU readiness flagged via do_not_do.
Synthetic cervical squamous cell carcinoma FIGO IIIB (with hydronephrosis resolved by ureteric stent). Engine should select cisplatin radiosensitization via ALGO-CERVICAL-LOCALLY-ADVANCED-1L step 1.
Synthetic GBM IDH-WT, MGMT-methylated, post gross-total resection, ECOG 1. Engine should select Stupp protocol via ALGO-GBM-NEWLY-DIAGNOSED-1L step 1.
Synthetic high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma FIGO IIIC, BRCA1-germline (HRD+). Engine should select carbo+pacli + olaparib maintenance via ALGO-OVARIAN-ADVANCED-1L step 1.

biomarkers.BIO-RHOA-G17V: RHOA G17V hotspot mutation — AITL hallmark (~60-70% of AITL cases); diagnostic confirmation when phenotype ambiguous; not a treatment-stratifier per se

biomarkers.BIO-HTLV-1: seropositive (HTLV-1 confirmatory Western blot/LIA + clonal HTLV-1 integration on Southern blot or qPCR equivalent — required by WHO classification for ATLL diagnosis)

biomarkers.BIO-HPV-STATUS: HPV-positive (typically HPV-16/18) — vast majority of cervical cancers; HPV status required as eligibility / etiology confirmation and informs prognosis (HPV+ better than HPV-) and post-treatment surveillance

biomarkers.BIO-HBV-STATUS: any value valid (categorical etiology gate; HBsAg+ or anti-HBc+ → entecavir prophylaxis before brentuximab; anti-CD30 ADC has documented HBV reactivation risk; continue ≥12 mo post-BV)

biomarkers.BIO-CD5-IHC: positive (CD5+) — part of CLL diagnostic immunophenotype panel (Matutes score)

biomarkers.BIO-MGMT-METHYLATION: MGMT methylation status (methylated / unmethylated) — required input; methylated patients have substantially better TMZ response (~21.7 mo vs ~12.7 mo OS) but Stupp protocol applies regardless

biomarkers.BIO-IDH1-MUTATION: IDH1 R132H mutation (or non-canonical IDH1/IDH2) — defines IDH-mutant astrocytoma / oligodendroglioma

biomarkers.BIO-AFP: AFP baseline (ng/mL) — required as risk-stratifier and treatment-response marker; AFP-high subgroup (>400) showed comparable atezo-bev OS benefit per IMbrave150 subgroup analysis

biomarkers.BIO-KIT: Activating mutation in exon 11 (e.g., L576P, K642E) or exon 13 — KIT amplification alone NOT predictive

biomarkers.BIO-HBV-STATUS: any value valid (categorical etiology gate; HBsAg+ or anti-HBc+ → entecavir prophylaxis mandatory before first daratumumab dose; anti-CD38 class effect — fulminant reactivation reported)

biomarkers.BIO-RET: RET mutation positive (somatic M918T OR germline MEN2-associated C634/M918T/other)

biomarkers.BIO-EBV-DNA: quantitative copies/mL (or IU/mL via WHO standard) on EDTA plasma; baseline + monitoring during therapy and post-treatment surveillance — NOT a treatment-decision gate, used as response surrogate

biomarkers.BIO-BRAF-V600E: BRAF V600E confirmed by NGS or PCR

biomarkers.BIO-FRA: high (≥75% of viable tumor cells with ≥2+ membrane staining by VENTANA FOLR1 RxDx)

biomarkers.BIO-GLEASON-ISUP: Gleason / ISUP grade group (1-5) — mandatory pathologic stratifier across all prostate cancer indications

biomarkers.BIO-HCV-STATUS: anti-HCV-positive (categorical screen — pairs with BIO-HCV-RNA confirmation; antibody alone insufficient for treatment decision)

biomarkers.BIO-CD52-IHC: positive (CD52+) — universal target of alemtuzumab; T-PLL is uniformly CD52-bright
